framework_name: DISA Kubernetes Security Technical Implementation Guide
description: Maps Sidereal probe types and their NIST 800-53 controls to Kubernetes STIG rule IDs. Only includes STIG rules where Sidereal provides operational validation beyond static configuration checks.

stig_rules.title: Must prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions
stig_rules.sidereal_validation: Probes attempt unauthorized API operations with non-privileged ServiceAccounts and verify they are denied. Validates RBAC enforcement, not just policy existence.

stig_rules.title: Must separate user functionality from management functionality
stig_rules.sidereal_validation: Probes verify namespace-level separation between user workloads and management-plane components.

stig_rules.title: Kubernetes dashboard must not be enabled
stig_rules.sidereal_validation: Probes continuously test reachability of the dashboard network endpoint. An unreachable endpoint provides stronger evidence than a static pod-presence check — it validates the boundary is enforced, not just that no pod is labeled kubernetes-dashboard.

stig_rules.title: API Server must enforce ports, protocols, services (PPS) registration process
stig_rules.sidereal_validation: Probes test whether traffic on unapproved ports is blocked by NetworkPolicy enforcement at the CNI layer.

stig_rules.title: Scheduler must enforce ports, protocols, services (PPS) registration process
stig_rules.sidereal_validation: Probes verify scheduler port exposure complies with PPS requirements.

stig_rules.title: Controller Manager must enforce ports, protocols, services (PPS) registration process
stig_rules.sidereal_validation: Probes verify controller-manager port exposure complies with PPS requirements.

stig_rules.title: etcd must enforce ports, protocols, services (PPS) registration process
stig_rules.sidereal_validation: Probes verify etcd port exposure complies with PPS requirements.

stig_rules.title: Cluster must use non-privileged host ports for user pods
stig_rules.sidereal_validation: Probes scan running pods for privileged port usage (< 1024) and verify enforcement.

stig_rules.title: API Server must have ValidatingAdmissionWebhook enabled
stig_rules.sidereal_validation: Probes verify the webhook is not just enabled but actively responding to admission requests.

stig_rules.title: Must have Pod Security Admission control file configured
stig_rules.sidereal_validation: Probes submit a non-compliant pod spec and verify it is rejected by the Pod Security Admission controller. Validates enforcement, not just configuration.

stig_rules.title: Must enforce Pod Security Standards or equivalent
stig_rules.sidereal_validation: Probes submit privileged container specs, hostPath mounts, and capability escalations to verify they are rejected. Tests the enforcement layer end-to-end.

stig_rules.title: Secrets must not be stored as environment variables
stig_rules.sidereal_validation: Probes enumerate pod specs across target namespaces for secretKeyRef in environment variables. Continuous validation catches new deployments that introduce violations.

stig_rules.title: Secrets must not be stored as environment variables
stig_rules.sidereal_validation: Cross-namespace secret access probes verify information flow enforcement. Secrets-as-env-vars represent an uncontrolled information flow.

stig_rules.title: API Server must generate audit records for all write operations
stig_rules.sidereal_validation: Detection probes fire a synthetic action and independently verify the audit/detection pipeline captured it. Validates the full audit chain end-to-end.

config_only_rules.description: STIG rules that are pure configuration checks. Sidereal's discovery engine may generate SiderealProbeRecommendation entries noting these are outside probe scope, but does not actively validate them. Tools like kube-bench, OpenSCAP, or the Compliance Operator are better suited for these checks.

categories.rationale: Static flag checks on API server, kubelet, etcd, scheduler. No behavioral validation needed.
File ownership and permission checks on node filesystem. Requires host-level access, not API-level probing.
Static process argument checks. Binary present-or-absent; no behavioral dimension.
Audit log path, size, retention, and backup flags. Config-level only. (V-242403, the operational audit validation rule, IS mapped above.)
SSH, kernel protection, dynamic kubelet config. Node-level checks outside Kubernetes API scope.
